summary: macOS-gated import-time backdoor with shell-command C2 over a pypi-lookalike domain
details: On import, pylogft/__init__.py checks whether the install path starts with /Users or /Library and, on macOS, silently spawns pylogft/_check.py as a detached subprocess. _check.py POSTs the absolute install directory (which typically embeds the local macOS username) to https://pypkg.dev/project/pylogft/json with TLS certificate verification explicitly disabled (ssl._create_unverified_context), then polls that endpoint every 60 seconds, base64-decodes each response, and passes the decoded value to os.system(f"pip show {package_list}"). The shell_escape filter permits the metacharacters `;`, `|`, `&`, and `>`, so the remote server can inject arbitrary shell commands that execute on the installer's machine. The C2 host pypkg.dev is a lookalike of pypi.org's project JSON URL shape, consistent with attacker C2 infrastructure rather than a legitimate publisher endpoint. The package advertises itself as a logging helper and has no legitimate reason to beacon to an external host or execute server-supplied commands.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
